title: NOC 72410 – Automotive service technicians, truck and bus mechanics and mechanical repairers | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Automotive and Truck Mechanics (NOC 72410) are the high-tech diagnosticians keeping Canada’s transport and retail sectors moving. As the country accelerates its shift toward Electric Vehicles (EVs) and zero-emission transit, these professionals have seen their roles evolve from purely mechanical repair to advanced electronics and software troubleshooting. Operating at the TEER 2 level, they are responsible for the safe operation of over 25 million registered vehicles. In 2026, the industry is facing a moderate risk of labour shortage, with over 52,600 job openings projected through 2033, primarily driven by the retirement of nearly 35% of the current workforce.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Usually requires college, apprenticeship, or 2+ years of experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officersSub-Major Group724 – Machine repair and maintenance trades

duties: Advanced Diagnostics: Using computerized diagnostic devices to isolate faults in mechanical, electrical, and electronic systems.
System Repair: Adjusting, repairing, or replacing parts in fuel systems, brakes, steering, suspension, and climate control.
Engine & Drive Train: Overhauling engines and transmissions to manufacturer specifications.
Commercial Maintenance: Adjusting and repairing commercial transport truck systems, including air brakes, hydraulic, and structural components.
Customer Advising: Discussing work with supervisors and advising customers on vehicle conditions and future repair requirements.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a four-year apprenticeship program OR a combination of over four years of work experience and industry courses is required for certification.
Certification: Trade certification is compulsory in Alberta, Ontario, New Brunswick, Prince Edward Island, and Nova Scotia. It is voluntary but highly recommended in all other provinces.
Specialized Licensing: In Ontario, certification from Skilled Trades Ontario is mandatory for seven specific sub-trades, including Truck and Coach Technician.
Red Seal: The Interprovincial Standards Red Seal is the "Gold Standard," allowing you to work in any province and access high-paying union and fleet roles.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Moderate to Good, with "Extreme Demand" for specialists in EV systems and heavy-duty commercial transport:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. In February and March 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for NOC 72410 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker and In-Demand Skills streams. Minimum scores for regional draws (Southwestern and Northern Ontario) started at 50–53, reflecting a critical need for mechanics in regional logistics hubs.

Alberta: Highest Median Wage. Alberta reports some of the highest earnings in the country, with a median wage of $35.00/hr and top-tier truck mechanics in the industrial sectors earning up to $51.00/hr.

British Columbia: Strong demand. Focused in the "Mainland/Southwest" region. BC projects over 4,900 openings through 2035. Automotive technicians are currently a priority for SkilledTradesBC certification.

National Trend: Over 94% of workers in this NOC are employed full-time. Approximately 17% are self-employed, significantly higher than the average for other trades, offering a clear path to business ownership.
Why Work in Canada as an Automotive or Truck Mechanic?
Lead the EV Transition: Gain hands-on experience with 2026-grade technologies, including high-voltage battery systems and regenerative braking.
Unbeatable Stability: Your skills are vital for essential services: public transit, municipal fleets, and the global supply chain.
Elite Compensation: As a TEER 2 trade, your median annual earnings are approximately $73,040, with specialized "Red Seal" technicians in major cities earning over $105,000.
Major 2026 PR Priority: On February 18, 2026, Canada launched a new Express Entry Transport Category specifically targeting NOC 72410, allowing for PR invitations at lower CRS thresholds.
pathways: Express Entry (2026 Transport Category): Launched on February 18, 2026, this category targets NOC 72410. Candidates require 12 months of qualifying experience (Canadian or international) within the last 3 years to receive an ITA.
OINP Employer Job Offer: In March 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72410 under the Foreign Worker and In-Demand Skills streams with scores starting at 50–53.
BC PNP Skilled Worker: A reliable route for technicians with a job offer from a BC-based dealership or fleet service, particularly in the Mainland/Southwest region.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A confirmed initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Automotive technicians are classified as "High-Priority Essential Workers."
